en.extract:
Charlotte's bulbul (Iole charlottae) is a species of songbird in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae.
It is found on Borneo in its natural habitat of subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests.
It is threatened by habitat loss.


Taxonomy and systematics
Charlotte's bulbul was originally described in the genus Criniger. It was split from the buff-vented bulbul by the IOC in 2017.

'''Charlotte's bulbul''' ('''''Iole charlottae''''') is a species of [[songbird]] in the [[bulbul]] family, Pycnonotidae.
It is found on [[Borneo]] in its natural [[habitat]] of subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s.
It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].

==Taxonomy and systematics==
Charlotte's bulbul was originally described in the genus ''[[Criniger]]''. It was split from the [[buff-vented bulbul]] by the [[International Ornithological Committee|IOC]] in 2017.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/updates/species-updates/|title=Species Updates « IOC World Bird List|website=www.worldbirdnames.org|language=en-US|access-date=2017-06-05}}</ref>
